name: Andrea Rodriguez
ai_summary: Andrea Rodriguez has 1.5 years of professional nanny experience with her 10-month-old daughter and children up to age 7. Located in Ogden UT with flexible FT/PT availability; she resolves conflicts through patient redirection and brings a nurturing passion for childcare.

The way that I would deal with conflict, especially beating children is redirecting. I found that redirecting is a very positive form of helping a child cope with their feelings and actually Express their feelings. I feel like children don't really know the way that they feel. And they kind of have those these little burst outs because they don't know what they feel. I feel like breaking things down and explain to them why it was wrong, and why it's hurting the other child's feelings and letting them Express, why they felt the need to put
 put hands on the other child than why they were so angry that allows them to take a few steps back and realize that maybe they aren't really angry or maybe they were just a little sad or maybe they were just a little hungry or maybe they just wanted attention and I feel like being able to get down to the root of why they're reacting the way that the reacting is a very important technique. Not only just that but redirecting like of course explaining to them that what they did was wrong but also allowing them how to feel.
 Need to them. Why it hurt the other person's feelings and then allowing them to hug it out. Even a, he Knuckles or high five. I feel like those little small things as well, gets the tension off of the child, which is very easy for a child to get overwhelmed with tension because they're so, they're so young and they're so new to feelings are exploring their own feelings, sometimes, they don't even know how they're feeling. So I feel like redirecting and also understanding the Child Actions, that goes a very long way.
